differential_diagnoses: Duodenal perforation (penetrating peptic ulcer) with secondary retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Lemierre's syndrome (variant with right renal vein/IVC involvement)
Secondary antiphospholipid syndrome due to infection
next_steps: Emergent surgical or gastroenterology consultation for EGD and potential intervention
Repeat imaging with EGD or focused CT scan with oral contrast to evaluate for duodenal perforation/fistula
Optimize antimicrobial therapy for S. anginosus and consider empiric coverage for potential anaerobic or mixed infection
Continued therapeutic anticoagulation for documented renal vein/IVC thrombosis
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Serial blood cultures to monitor for clearance of bacteremia
Interdisciplinary consultation with Interventional Radiology for potential drainage of suspected retroperitoneal or perirenal collection

differential_diagnoses: Penetrating duodenal ulcer with localized retroperitoneal abscess and septic thrombophlebitis
Secondary (infectious) antiphospholipid syndrome causing portal/renal vein thrombosis
next_steps: CT scan of the abdomen with water-soluble oral contrast to confirm duodenal perforation/fistula
General surgery consultation for consideration of surgical source control and drainage
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out infective endocarditis
Serial blood cultures to ensure clearance of S. anginosus
Strongyloides serology and stool evaluation for larvae
Continued targeted antibiotic therapy for S. anginosus with focus on anaerobic coverage
